decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85.0°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. This is the highest-priority lever for improving lateral heat spreading and reducing peak temperature.

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader conductivity within bounds enhances lateral heat diffusion away from the chip, lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution in the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches very high values relative to other materials
No geometric conflicts expected as this is a material property change
